auth.title: Login
auth.subtitle: Welcome back. Enter your admin credentials to continue.

dunning.title: Dunning Queue
dunning.subtitle: Standalone live recovery queue powered by the same Revenue Ops data.

states.loadFailed: Failed to load — make sure you are logged in.

queue.title: Active Dunning Queue
queue.empty: No active dunning — all subscriptions are healthy.

experiments.title: A/B Tests
experiments.subtitle: Manage real database-backed experiments stored in ab_tests and ab_test_arms, and monitor the live aggregate stats recorded for each experiment.

table.title: Live Experiments
table.description: These rows come from the backend ab_tests, ab_test_arms, ab_test_assignments, and ab_test_arm_stats tables.
table.name: Experiment

form.title: Create A/B Experiment
form.description: This form writes directly to ab_tests and ab_test_arms. It intentionally captures only the fields that are truly persisted today.
form.name: Experiment Name
form.descriptionPlaceholder: What are you testing and why?

experimentStudio.title: Experiment Studio
experimentStudio.subtitle: Live control room for persisted experiments. This page intentionally focuses on orchestration, health, and runtime visibility instead of pretending a full visual builder already exists.

selector.title: Selected Experiment
selector.description: Pick any persisted experiment to inspect its lifecycle, saved arm plan, and live runtime availability.

lifecycle.title: Lifecycle
lifecycle.description: Saved timestamps and scheduling state for the selected experiment.

capabilities.title: Runtime Capability Matrix
capabilities.description: Each row below reflects an actual backend endpoint call in the current environment.

arms.title: Saved Arm Plan
arms.description: These arms come from the persisted admin experiments table. Live win probabilities are shown only when the bandit statistics endpoint is available.

runtime.title: Runtime Signals
runtime.description: Best-effort runtime indicators from bandit endpoints that are already wired.
runtime.banditNote: Bandit statistics are live, but some advanced routes may still be partially wired in the backend runtime.
runtime.classicNote: This experiment is not bandit-enabled, so Studio only shows the persisted experiment plan and generic service health.

notes.title: Operational Notes
notes.description: Studio explains what is actually wired today and where to perform supported actions.
notes.builderBody: Use A/B Tests to create persisted experiments. Studio is not pretending to be a draft editor with save/launch mutations that do not exist yet.
notes.banditBody: Use Bandit Model for dedicated bandit telemetry. Studio surfaces only the minimum runtime status needed for orchestration.
notes.advancedBody: Objective scoring and sliding-window routes are shown exactly as they behave in the current runtime, including failures or placeholder responses.
notes.readOnly: Read-only by design for now. This page is a truthful control room, not a fake workflow builder.

states.loadFailed: Failed to load experiment studio data.
states.emptyBody: Create your first experiment in A/B Tests before using Studio.

bandit.title: Bandit Model
bandit.subtitle: Read-only monitoring for live bandit experiments using persisted experiment arms, Thompson statistics, and engine metrics.

selector.title: Tracked Experiment
selector.description: Pick any experiment marked as bandit-enabled. This screen only shows routes that are already wired in the backend.

arms.title: Arm Performance
arms.description: Win probability comes from the live Monte Carlo statistics endpoint. Traffic weight reflects the saved experiment configuration, not a hidden runtime override.

metrics.title: Engine Metrics
metrics.description: These values come from the advanced bandit metrics endpoint when available.
metrics.note: The backend currently exposes balance index and pending rewards most reliably. More advanced knobs like exploration rate are not admin-configurable from this page yet.

config.title: Saved Experiment Config
config.description: This page reflects persisted experiment settings but does not pretend to support pause/stop/save actions that are not wired yet.
config.readOnlyNotice: Read-only for now: use A/B Tests to create experiments. Runtime bandit assignments and rewards continue to flow through the existing backend endpoints.

states.emptyBody: Create an experiment with bandit mode enabled in A/B Tests before using this page.

feedback.title: Delayed Feedback
feedback.subtitle: Live operator console for delayed conversions. This page surfaces what the backend actually supports today instead of pretending there is a survey/form builder behind it.

selector.title: Tracked Experiment
selector.description: Select any bandit-enabled experiment to inspect the delayed-feedback runtime around it.

probes.title: Delayed Feedback Endpoints
probes.description: Each row below reflects either a real runtime GET probe or an explicitly manual-only mutation path.

process.title: Manual Conversion Processing
process.description: This form sends a real delayed conversion request to the backend. Use it only when you intend to process a live transaction.
process.warning: No dry-run exists today. Submitting this form calls the live conversion endpoint immediately.

arms.title: Experiment Arm Context
arms.description: The table below uses live bandit statistics when available so operators can see what conversion processing would affect.

notes.title: Operational Notes
notes.description: This page documents the current delayed-feedback runtime exactly as it exists today.
notes.pendingBody: The backend repository supports pending rewards, but the currently exposed read endpoints are not yet returning live reward records in this runtime path.
notes.conversionBody: The conversion endpoint is real. If delayed feedback is disabled in the current backend runtime, the response will say so instead of faking success.
notes.operatorBody: Use this page to inspect support status and manually process a real conversion when you already have trusted transaction and user identifiers.

states.loadFailed: Failed to load delayed feedback data.
states.emptyBody: Create a bandit-enabled experiment before using Delayed Feedback.

feedback.loadFailed: Failed to refresh delayed feedback data.

slidingWindow.title: Sliding Window
slidingWindow.subtitle: Live runtime view for sliding-window support. This page surfaces what the backend window routes actually do today instead of pretending configuration mutations already exist.

selector.title: Tracked Experiment
selector.description: Select any bandit-enabled experiment to inspect sliding-window route availability around it.

probes.title: Window Endpoint Matrix
probes.description: Each row reflects a real runtime probe or an explicitly manual-only mutation path.

arms.title: Experiment Arm Context
arms.description: The table below uses live bandit statistics when available so operators can see the experiment state adjacent to sliding-window operations.

notes.title: Operational Notes
notes.description: This page documents the current sliding-window runtime exactly as it exists today.
notes.strategyBody: The repository contains a real sliding-window strategy and maintenance jobs, but admin-facing runtime routes are only useful if they are correctly wired in the running API.
notes.readRoutesBody: Window info and event export are probed directly from the live backend. If they fail, this page shows the real response instead of fake window metrics.
notes.trimBody: Trim is intentionally not auto-called from a read-only page load. The route exists in code, but this screen does not pretend it is safe or fully wired for operators yet.

readiness.title: Window Readiness
readiness.description: A compact summary of how usable the sliding-window API is in the current runtime.
readiness.body: Use Bandit Model for live probability telemetry. Sliding Window only reports whether the dedicated window routes are truly usable in this environment.

states.loadFailed: Failed to load sliding window data.
states.emptyBody: Create a bandit-enabled experiment before using Sliding Window.

feedback.loadFailed: Failed to refresh sliding window data.

multiObjective.title: Multi-Objective
multiObjective.subtitle: Live runtime view for hybrid objective support. This page reports what the backend objective routes and strategy wiring actually support today instead of pretending a full optimizer workflow already exists.

selector.title: Tracked Experiment
selector.description: Select any bandit-enabled experiment to inspect hybrid objective route availability around it.

probes.title: Objective Endpoint Matrix
probes.description: Each row reflects a real runtime probe or an explicitly manual-only mutation path.

catalog.title: Supported Objective Model
catalog.description: These objective types come from the backend hybrid strategy and experiment configuration model.

conversion.label: Conversion
conversion.body: Base Thompson Sampling reward objective. Used as the fallback and one of the hybrid dimensions.

ltv.label: LTV
ltv.body: Expected value objective based on conversion probability multiplied by average lifetime value.

revenue.label: Revenue
revenue.body: Revenue-weighted objective derived from conversion probability and normalized revenue per sample.

hybrid.label: Hybrid
hybrid.body: Weighted combination of conversion, LTV, and revenue. Backend defaults are 50% / 30% / 20% when hybrid is enabled without explicit weights.

context.title: Experiment Arm Context
context.description: The table below uses live bandit statistics when available so operators can inspect experiment state adjacent to objective scoring.

scores.title: Live Objective Scoreboard
scores.description: If the objective scores route is actually wired in the current runtime, the table below shows returned per-arm scores.
scores.unavailable: Objective scores are not available in the current runtime. This page does not fabricate Pareto fronts or hybrid rankings when the backend route fails.

notes.title: Operational Notes
notes.description: This page documents the current multi-objective runtime exactly as it exists today.
notes.strategyBody: The backend repository contains a real HybridObjectiveStrategy and advanced engine support for objective reward updates.
notes.runtimeBody: Objective score availability is probed directly from the live backend. If the route fails for the current experiment, this page shows that failure instead of fake optimization output.
notes.configBody: The admin experiments payload currently does not expose persisted objective config to this screen, and config mutation is intentionally not auto-triggered from a read-only page load.

readiness.title: Objective Readiness
readiness.description: A compact summary of how usable the multi-objective API is in the current runtime.
readiness.body: Use Bandit Model for live probability telemetry. Multi-Objective only reports whether dedicated objective scoring is truly usable in this environment.

states.emptyBody: Create a bandit-enabled experiment before using Multi-Objective.

matomo.title: Matomo Analytics
matomo.subtitle: Open the real Matomo dashboard using the saved platform settings. This page intentionally avoids fabricating KPI cards when only integration config is available.

connection.title: Connection Details
connection.description: These values come from the saved admin platform settings. The auth token is never rendered to the browser; this page only exposes whether one is present.
connection.behaviorBody: Use Open Matomo for the real dashboard. The embed below is best-effort and depends on your Matomo instance allowing iframe embedding and your current Matomo login session.

embed.title: Live Dashboard Embed
embed.description: The embed URL is built from the saved Matomo URL and Site ID for the selected date range.
embed.notConfiguredBody: Set Matomo URL and Site ID in Settings before opening the live dashboard from this screen.
embed.bestEffortNotice: If the iframe is blocked by Matomo or your browser session is not authenticated there, use the Open Matomo button instead.

pricing.title: Pricing Tiers
pricing.subtitle: Manage live pricing tiers stored in the admin database.

table.title: Live Pricing Tiers
table.description: These rows are loaded from and persisted to the backend pricing_tiers table.
table.name: Name

form.description: Define the commercial copy, price points, and feature list for each tier.
form.name: Tier Name

descriptions.general: Load and persist the core platform configuration used by the admin workspace.
descriptions.integrations: Store provider credentials and tracking configuration in one place.
descriptions.notifications: Choose which operational events should notify the admin team.
descriptions.security: Persist safe security preferences and rotate the admin password securely.

winback.title: Winback Campaigns
winback.subtitle: Launch real winback offers for recently cancelled users and monitor grouped offer outcomes by campaign ID.

table.title: Live Winback Campaigns
table.description: These rows are grouped live from the backend winback_offers table by campaign_id.

form.title: Launch Winback Campaign
form.description: This form maps directly to the backend winback service and creates offers for users with recently cancelled subscriptions.
